forms_variables:
DS001_3

MH001 Medical History
Preferred Term


CDS01-RF02
logic_summary:
For randomized subjects with the Date of Randomization entered in Randomization (DS001_3) page,
compare their Medical History(MH001) preferred terms with the list of anti-TNF- α (adalimumab, infliximab,
golimumab, certolizumab pegol), IL-17 inhibitor (secukinumab,bimekizumab, ixekizumab) or IL-23 inhibitors
(ustekinumab, risankizumab, tildrakizumab, guselkimab).
If a subject had taken anti-TNF- α within 12 weeks of first study dose, or had taken IL-17 inhibitor within 16
weeks of first study dose, or had taken IL-23 inhibitors within 6 months of first study dose, the subject will be
considered incompliant.
Divide the number of incompliant subjects by the total number of randomized subjects.

iqmp_risk_id: SR-04586 Statistical Validity participants who are <4/7 days compliant with HSSDD and Skin Pain NRS CDS01-RF02
forms_variables:

Variable: Diary Date

Completion Status
Dataset: [DS001_3] Randomization
Variable: Date of Randomization
Dataset: Oral Dosing eDiary
Variable: Did you take your study medication as instructed?


CDS01-RF02
logic_summary:
For subjects with Date of Randomization entered in [DS001_3] Randomization page, and in any of
the Oral Dosing eDiary pages, with the question “Did you take your study medication as
instructed?” answered as Yes:
Calculate their HSSDD and Skin Pain NRS compliance in each week. If in any week the subject
had fewer than 4/7 HSSDD or Skin Pain NRS reported, then flag the subject as incompliant.
In the output, calculate the proportion of incompliant subjects among all randomized and dosed
subjects.

iqmp_risk_id: SR-04081 Medicinal Product Administration participants who are <80% compliant with IP dosing
forms_variables:
Dataset: [DS001_3] Randomization
Variable: Date of Randomization
Dataset: Oral Dosing eDiary

logic_summary:
For subjects with Date of Randomization entered in [DS001_3] Randomization page:
Compute their dosing compliance according to the Oral Dosing eDiary by calculating the proportion
of the total number of days in which “No” was reported for the question “Did you take your study
medication as instructed?” among the number of the entire dosing period. If the proportion is
smaller than 80%, then flag the subject as incompliant
In the output, calculate the proportion of incompliant subjects among all randomized subjects.

If a subject had taken anti-TNF- α within 12 weeks of first study dose, or had taken IL-17 inhibitor within 16
weeks of first study dose, or had taken IL-23 inhibitors within 6 months of first study dose, the subject will be
considered incompliant.
Divide the number of incompliant subjects by the total number of randomized subjects.

iqmp_risk_id: Risk ID: no specific risk ID, as per IQMP to 0 or 30 minutes Rationale: Enhanced Data Quality Check, (KRI_ROUNDED_TIME_DOSE) looking for questionable data, scientific misconduct, potentially fraudulent activity
forms_variables:
TREATMENT (ONCE EC001_4_3; EVERY WEEK: EC001_4_1 and EC001_4_4; EVERY TWO WEEKS: EC001_4_2,
EC001_4_5).
ECDOSE_001
ECSTDTC_001
logic_summary:
- When the dose (ECDOSE_001) is 0 or null, then the record is not considered (neither counted as rounded
nor not rounded).
- When the time (ECSTDTC_001) was not reported by the site, then the record is not considered (neither
counted as rounded nor not rounded).
- When the dose (ECDOSE_001) is not null and not 0 (i.e > 0), IP administration time will be counted as
rounded if the minutes from ECSTDTC_001 end in 0 or 30.
When dose (ECDOSE_001) is not null and not 0 (i.e. >) 0, IP administration time will be counted as not
rounded if the minutes from ECSTDTC_001 do not end in 0 or 30.
Numerator: number of rounded times of IP administration (rounded to 0 or 30 minutes) for doses
administered
Denominator: number of times of IP administrations reported, for doses administered.
corrective_action:
Study team to discuss. CRO to review with site whether time is accurate, or may be falsified (i.e. potentially
inaccurate source) and retrain site, if applicable.
comment:
KRI added at KRI F/U19 analysis, as part of the Enhanced Data Quality Check.

- Calculate the number of days between the date of first IP administration and the last dose reported in
the CRF (including doses reported as 0): Date (ECSTDTC_001) of Last IP Administration (any dose, except
when ECDOSE_001_ND is not null) minus Date (ECSTDTC_001) of First IP Administration (ECDOSE_001 >
0) + 1 day*
Statistical Mean Calculated for Each Site
[*note: + 1 day is added to the calculation in order so that the time on treatment for subject having just one
dose will be 1 day rather than 0 day].
corrective_action: Study team to discuss. CRO to review with site.

logic_summary:
KRI will look at all randomized subjects. KRI will apply an event rate test on a user defined variable called
CP_MISSED_IMWG. This variable will be flagged as 1 if the following conditions are met:
• A visit where the IMWG assessment is scheduled according to protocol’s SoA.
• This visit is not erroneous and has a valid DOV (visit is performed).
• The Date of disease assessment [RSDTC_041] is missing. OR site entered a form level comment
of NOT DONE, UNKNOWN OR NOT APPLICABLE.
• The snapshot date is more than 21 days of the date of visit.
CP_MISSED_IMWG will be marked as 0 if the conditions described above are not met.
corrective_action:
Investigate individual reasons for Not Done IMWG assessments and if necessary, contact the site to
discuss potential root cause for mitigation & correction.

prophylaxis","COVID-19 treatment","SARS-CoV-2 carrier","SARS-CoV-2 test false negative","SARS-CoV-2
antibody test positive","SARS-CoV-2 sepsis","SARS-CoV-2 viraemia”, Multisystem inflammatory syndrome
in children”, “Congenital COVID-19","Vaccine derived SARS-CoV-2 infection","SARS-CoV-2 RNA
increased","SARS-CoV-2 RNA decreased","SARS-CoV-2 RNA fluctuation”, “Post-acute COVID-19
syndrome”, “Multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults”, “Multisystem inflammatory syndrome”,
“Thrombosis with thrombocytopenia syndrome”, “Breakthrough COVID-19"

logic_summary:
For randomized subjects only.
Select the SCR QTCF records and those not in screening. Remove also blank SCR QTCF records. Merging the non-
SCR QTCF and SCR QTCF results. KRI will calculate the QTCF gap compared with baseline and flag the abnormal
QTCF records. Specifically in our study based the protocol (Section 10.9) we have QTcF prolongation to >500 ms.
Absolute value of QTcF >450 ms AND QTcF change from baseline >60 ms. We will flag pts with QTcF change from
baseline >60 ms.

logic_summary:
Blood samples (3 serum aliquots) are to be collected from dosed (when NODONESTATE of EC001_2 and
[EC001_2_1 form is 0) subjects during V1_DAY1_VAX1, V2_MONTH1, V4_MONTH3.
If a subject has completed any of these visits but have fewer than 3 aliquots
collected, that subject-visit will be flagged as Non-Compliant, else Compliant.
It is to be analysed only for subjects that have been randomized and dosed in the study.
Each subject visit will be marked per the following criteria:
1. Sample Type [BESCAT_012] = 'SERUM' and number of aliquots is fewer than 3 during the expected
visit (when completed) will be considered Non-Compliant.
2. Sample Type [BESCAT_012] = 'SERUM' and number of aliquots is 3 or more during the expected
visit (when completed), will be considered Compliant

iqmp_risk_id: SR-00507 Sample ID Bulk Entry (KRI_SAMPLE_BULK) CDS01-RF02 C3671059
logic_summary:
List of unique samples (ITEM_QUESTION= "Sample ID:" with ITEMSET_INDEX being not NULL)
for their initial entries (MODIFICATION_TYPE = 'Initial Entry') from the Audit Trail for Electronic
Sample Tracking (ABBREVIATED_FORM_NAME contains SAMP). List of samples with their ID
length (CURRENT_VALUE) = 6, are to be included in the analysis.
These samples from Audit Trail will then be joined with SM_ BEETRK001 (where BEOCCUR_001
= ‘YES’ and BEDTC_001 is not NULL) using the Sample ID to calculate the sample collection and
entry difference.
Above data will be used to”
1. Evaluate the average data entry time between when the sample was collected and entered
in the database. Samples are expected to be entered within 4 days.
2. If there are at least 3 late entries on any given date (samples entered in the database on the
same date), calculate the entry gap between each pair of samples (difference between the
database entry of one sample to the other).
The subjects should be CP_POPULATION_NAME <> "ALL" (Screen Fails are excluded).
comment:
Study Management will be assigned to review this signal in CluePoints per study team’s
agreement.

iqmp_risk_id: SR-01614 (IRMS ID) Immunogenicity (KRI_MISSED_SAMPLE_TRACKING)
logic_summary:
For vaccinated subjects.
Serum samples. (BEETRK012 where BESCAT_012 = "SERUM" )
Sample Collection visits: 'V1_DAY1_VAX','V2_WEEK2_FUP', 'V3_MONTH1', 'V4_MONTH6’,
‘V201_DAY1_VAX’, V202_MONTH1’
For ages (Age will be used for Aliquot counting):
Cohort 1:
- 50mL is expected/collected throughout except for V4 where only 20 mL is expected.
Cohort 2:
- 12 - 17, 10mL is expected/collected.
- >= 18, 50mL is expected/collected.
Number of Aliquots expected:
- 10mL, 2 SEND and 1 RETAIN are expected --> 3 total
- 20mL, 3 SEND and 3 RETAIN are expected --> 6 total
- 50mL, 5 SEND and 4 RETAIN are expected --> 9 total
Immunogenicity Samples are expected during above visits from vaccinated subjects. If subject completes a
visit but is missing a sample or fewer than sufficient number of aliquots collected, the subject-visit will be
flagged as MISSING. But if a subject has completed one of the above visits and has also samples with
sufficient number of aliquots collected, the subject-visit will be flagged as COLLECTED / NOT MISSING.


CDS01-RF02
corrective_action:
Name: High Missed Immunogenicity Samples
Actions
Action description: Discuss with the site high number of missed Immunogenicity samples or few aliquots
collected and investigate the root-cause. Take any further appropriate actions to mitigate the issue.

data in the forms below:

CDS01-RF02

3. For subjects who discontinued treatment and were assessed as having progressive disease (PD) by the

discontinuation are not required to be entered into InForm.

reconciliation.

by the principal investigator (PI), tumor assessments conducted after PD and after treatment
discontinuation will not be counted in this KRI.
